commit.message:
fix(db): detect mirrored/custom-named pool drives for hybrid mode (#53)

* fix(plugin): replace free-text bind address with validated dropdown

- Replace free-text bind address input with <select> dropdown offering
  127.0.0.1 (local only) and 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces), preventing
  users from entering invalid IPs that break the daemon
- Fix PHP proxy (vault_target_url) to use actual bind address instead
  of hardcoded 127.0.0.1, resolving UI showing daemon as stopped when
  bound to a non-loopback address
- Fix Default button: replace non-functional Unraid #default mechanism
  with AJAX reset-config action in control.php that resets vault.cfg
  to defaults and restarts the daemon
- Add bind address validation in rc.vault (validate_bind_address) and
  apply.sh to reject IPs not bound to a local interface, falling back
  to 127.0.0.1 with a warning
- Sanitize config values in reset-config to prevent INI injection
- Remove obsolete API key warning from bind address help text
- Add migration option for existing custom bind addresses to standard
  options via a third dropdown entry

* feat(plugin): auto-detect NICs for bind address dropdown

- Add vault_get_local_ips() PHP function that parses ip -4 addr show
  to discover all IPv4 addresses and their interface names
- Populate bind address dropdown with detected NIC addresses (e.g.
  192.168.20.21 (br0), 172.17.0.1 (docker0), 192.168.122.1 (virbr0))
  alongside the static 127.0.0.1 and 0.0.0.0 options
- Deduplicate IPs that appear on multiple virtual/shim interfaces
- Show network-accessible warning for any non-loopback bind address,
  not just 0.0.0.0
- Existing rc.vault and apply.sh validation already accepts any local
  interface IP via ip addr show grep

* docs(settings): update UX labels and snapshot path restart notices

* fix(db): add path validation to SnapshotManager to prevent path traversal (CWE-22)

Add validateSnapshotPath defense-in-depth validation using filepath.Clean,
filepath.Abs, and '..' component rejection. Applied consistently to:
- SaveSnapshot (CodeQL alert #28)
- SetSnapshotPath (CodeQL alert #27)
- RestoreFromSnapshot
- RestoreFromPath
- SetUSBBackupPath / saveUSBBackup

Also fix data race in SaveSnapshot reading snapshotPath without mutex.

Closes #27, closes #28

* fix(db): detect mirrored/custom-named pool drives for hybrid mode

Replace hardcoded /mnt/cache references with dynamic pool discovery.
Systems with mirrored SSD cache pools (e.g., /mnt/nvme) are now
correctly detected for Database Location and Temporary Work Area.

- Add internal/unraid package with DiscoverPools() and PreferredPool()
- Use exclusion-based detection to enumerate /mnt/ pool mount points
- Prefer /mnt/cache for backwards compatibility, fall back to first pool
- Update daemon hybrid mode to use detected pool path
- Update tempdir staging cascade to include all discovered pools
- Update diagnostics collector to detect hybrid mode from any pool
- Update browse handler to show all pool drives dynamically
- Update uninstall cleanup to use discovered pool paths

Closes #49

* docs(changelog): add mirrored cache pool detection entries (closes #49)

* fix(review): address CodeRabbit review feedback

- tempdir: replace sync.Once with mutex+flag to avoid permanently caching
  empty pool discovery results (pools may not be mounted at early startup)
- uninstall_cleanup: only override DefaultSnapshotDB when --snapshot-path
  flag is explicitly set, preserving pool-aware default from PreferredPool()
- snapshot: use validated path in RestoreFromPath error message
- pools_test: convert to table-driven subtests per repo convention

* fix(review): address PR #53 review comments

- validateSnapshotPath: check '..' BEFORE filepath.Clean to actually
  prevent path traversal (CWE-22), update test to expect error
- diagnostics: only report hybrid mode when preferred pool is mounted,
  matching daemon startup behaviour (add unraid.IsMountedPool helper)
- rc.vault + apply.sh: use grep -F for fixed-string IP matching to
  prevent regex wildcard false positives; add ::1/:: to apply.sh
- control.php: add CSRF token validation for state-changing actions
- api.php: map ::1 bind to [::1] instead of 127.0.0.1 for IPv6 support
- Settings.svelte: remove incorrect 'restart required' messaging, add
  Apply button for manual snapshot path entry, consistent toast text
- CHANGELOG.md: consolidate duplicate ### headings under [Unreleased],
  update CWE-22 entry to reflect pre-clean traversal rejection

* fix(security): harden apply.sh, control.php, and snapshot path validation

- snapshot.go: use filepath.ToSlash before splitting on '/' so '..'
  detection works regardless of OS path separator
- apply.sh: replace 'source $CONFIG' with safe grep/sed key extraction
  to prevent arbitrary code execution from user-editable config
- apply.sh: check sed -i exit status and abort on failure
- control.php: remove $_GET fallback for CSRF token (POST-only)
- control.php: add backslash to INI sanitisation regex to prevent
  backslash-escape attacks on quoted values

* fix(plugin): strip single quotes from BIND_ADDRESS in apply.sh

The sed expression only removed double quotes, so a config value like
BIND_ADDRESS='0.0.0.0' would retain the single quotes and fail
validation. Now strips both single and double quotes.

* fix(plugin): harden control.php, apply.sh, and Vault.page

- control.php: reject non-POST requests for state-changing actions (405)
- apply.sh: strip trailing content after closing quote in BIND_ADDRESS
  to handle inline comments or whitespace
- Vault.page: add $var fallback initialisation from state/var.ini and
  use json_encode + encodeURIComponent for CSRF token escaping

* fix(plugin): use LOCK_EX for atomic config write in control.php

* fix(review): address review thread 4080118743 feedback

- Settings.svelte: Remove misleading 'takes effect on restart' from reset toast
- Vault.page: Add IPv6 loopback (::1) and wildcard (::) to bind address dropdown
- api.php: Expand vault_get_local_ips() to include IPv6 addresses
- rc.vault: Replace 'source' with safe grep/sed key parsing to prevent code injection
- control.php: Constrain SERVICE to allowlist; use single-quoted values; sanitize SNAPSHOT_PATH
- pools.go: Use /proc/self/mountinfo for mount detection instead of ReadDir heuristic

Per-slot tone targets: lower of_object pop target for thin tail

of_object has only 24 fillers at freq>=10 (vs 85+ for the other slots),
so its pop target is 1.0 instead of 1.5. Now --tone pop produces
recognizable of-objects like 'happiness', 'dissent', 'World War II'
instead of pulling from the niche tail.

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-12): address PR #754 review findings

Addresses Claude's review comments on PR #754:

Performance
- Pass pre-computed `tiered` result into `resolveStaticCall` as optional
  `tieredOverride` parameter, eliminating the duplicate `ctx.resolve(className,
  currentFile)` on every constructor call path.
- Cache `freeFormHasClassTarget` in `resolveCallTarget` so the S0 fast path
  and the free-form constructor retry share a single `.some()` scan.

Architecture
- Reconcile `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES` (call-processor) with `CLASS_TYPES`
  (symbol-table): `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES = [...CLASS_TYPES, 'Impl']`. This makes
  the relationship explicit — the call resolver's set is a strict superset
  of the heritage-index set, guaranteeing anything reachable via
  `lookupClassByName` also passes the resolver filter. Trait is now included
  (harmless: traits have no Constructor nodes, so step-3 returns undefined
  and step-5 still returns the class-like node when unique). Documented
  the Interface inclusion rationale (static methods + MRO walker).
- Collapse `resolveStaticCall`'s `methodName` parameter into `className` —
  all call sites passed identical values. Named constructors (Dart
  `User.fromJson()`) arrive as member calls and go through
  `resolveMemberCall`. Documented the reserved path for when a language
  surfaces a static-method-shaped call with a distinct member name.
- Document the known gap: `callForm === 'member'` constructor patterns
  (e.g. Python `models.User()`) are handled by the tail fallback, not S0.

Tests
- Add tiered-override test asserting `ctx.resolve` is not re-invoked when
  a pre-computed result is passed in.
- Add language-specific `_resolveCallTargetForTesting` integration tests
  for Java (`new User()`), Python (`User()`), and Kotlin (`User()`).

Verification: 3031 unit + 1766 integration tests pass, zero regressions.

* fix(SM-12): restrict resolveStaticCall fallback to instantiable kinds

Addresses the high-severity finding from the Codex adversarial review of
PR #754: `resolveStaticCall`'s step-5 "return the class itself when no
Constructor node is found" fallback reused `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES`, which —
after SM-11 and PR #754's reconciliation — now includes `Interface`,
`Trait`, and `Impl`. That is the method-dispatch set, not the
instantiable set, so constructor-shaped calls could resolve to
non-instantiable nodes and emit false `CALLS` edges.

Concrete failure: Rust same-file `impl User { ... }` alongside
`struct User { ... }` — both land at same-file tier, the Impl is not
filtered out, and the step-5 fallback produces a `CALLS` edge to the
`Impl` block instead of the `Struct`. The same widening exposed
Interface / Trait targets in Java / C# / PHP / Scala.

Fix
- Introduce `INSTANTIABLE_CLASS_TYPES = {'Class', 'Struct', 'Record'}`
  as a sibling to `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES`, documenting the contract
  explicitly and cross-referencing `CONSTRUCTOR_TARGET_TYPES`.
- Update `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES` JSDoc to clarify it is the method-dispatch
  set and add an anti-pattern warning against reusing it for
  constructor-fallback filtering.
- Tighten `resolveStaticCall` step 5: filter `classCandidates` through
  `INSTANTIABLE_CLASS_TYPES` before the `length === 1` check. This
  strips `Impl` from the Rust shadowing scenario (leaving `Struct` as
  the sole instantiable target) and null-routes Interface / Trait /
  `Impl`-alone scenarios, matching the SM-10 R3 null-route precedent.
- Step 3 (explicit Constructor lookup via `lookupMethodByOwner`) is
  intentionally unchanged — its `def.type === 'Constructor'` check is
  the correct contract, and legitimate Constructor nodes attached to
  `Impl` owners still resolve correctly.

Tests (+10 regression scenarios)
- Positive guards: Struct, Record fallback paths.
- Null-route: Interface (Java/C#/TS), PHP Trait, Rust Trait.
- Rust same-file shadowing: Struct wins over Impl.
- Rust Impl-alone: null-routes (no Struct present).
- Step-3 preservation: Constructor owned by Impl still resolves to the
  Constructor node, proving step-5 tightening doesn't leak into step 3.
- Full cascade via `_resolveCallTargetForTesting` for Interface and
  Trait — confirms no downstream path silently re-introduces the edge.

Verification
- `tsc --noEmit` clean
- 3041 unit tests pass (+10)
- 1766 integration tests pass
- Zero regressions

Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-09-002-fix-sm12-constructor-fallback-instantiable-only-plan.md
Codex review job: review-mnrao7fr-nv9y0e

* fix(SM-12): address PR #754 second review round

Addresses the 9 findings from the follow-up review on PR #754.

Performance
- Align `freeFormHasClassTarget` with `INSTANTIABLE_CLASS_TYPES`: drop
  `Enum` (S0 would always return null for it — wasted lookup work) and
  add `Record` (C# records and Kotlin data classes were bypassing S0
  entirely). The trigger set and the fallback filter set now agree by
  construction, documented inline.

Documentation
- Remove stale single-line JSDoc on `CLASS_LIKE_TYPES` (line 57) that
  duplicated the full multi-line block immediately below it — tooling
  picks up the first block so the old one-liner was shadowing the
  current explanation.
- Rewrite the `resolveStaticCall` JSDoc step list to match the actual
  step boundaries in the implementation (steps 3, 4, 5 were blurred in
  the old description).
- Add inline comment on step 3 documenting the same-name lookup
  assumption (`${candidate.nodeId}\0${className}`) and the symmetric
  miss case for Python `__init__`-style constructors.
- Add inline comment on step 4 documenting that it also catches the
  ambiguous-step-3 case, and warning against removing the check
  without handling that path explicitly.
- Add inline comment on step 5 enumerating the three length outcomes

commit.message:
fix(graphs): support passing a graph object directly to GraphExporter (#1032)

## Description

Fixes a logic error in `GraphExporter.__init__` where `GraphCreator` was
unconditionally called regardless of the input type, overwriting any
graph already loaded from a `.pt` file. Extended the fix to handle all
supported input types for the `graph` argument.

## What problem does this change solve?

This is a bugfix. The original code had `GraphCreator(...).create()`
outside the conditional block, meaning it was always executed — even
when a `.pt` file was provided and the graph had already been loaded via
`torch.load`. This silently discarded the loaded graph and attempted to
recreate it from a path that is not a recipe file.

The fix restructures `__init__` to correctly handle all supported input
types:

| Input type | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `HeteroData` | Used directly (no graph creation) |
| `str`/`Path` ending `.pt` | Loaded via `torch.load` |
| `str`/`Path` ending `.yaml`/`.yml` | Built via `GraphCreator` |
| `dict`/`DotDict`/`DictConfig` | Built via `GraphCreator` |

Additional improvements:
- Switched from `str.endswith()` to `Path.suffix` to safely handle both
`str` and `Path` inputs without raising `AttributeError`
- Updated the type annotation of `graph` to `str | Path | HeteroData |
dict | DotDict | DictConfig` to reflect all supported types
- Added imports for `DotDict` (from `anemoi.utils.config`) and
`DictConfig` (from `omegaconf`)
- Updated the error message to mention `.yml` as a supported extension

## What issue or task does this change relate to?

##  Additional notes ##

Minimal single-file change (`graphs/src/anemoi/graphs/export.py`). No
behavioural change for existing `.yaml` string inputs.

commit.message:
fix: disable auto-generated Info.plist and set UIApplicationSupportsMultipleScenes to false

Root cause: Xcode was auto-generating Info.plist during build (GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE = YES),
which ignored our custom Info.plist settings. This caused iOS 18+ to apply multitasking
window constraints on iPhone 17, resulting in ~240px black bars top/bottom.

Two-part fix:
1. Set GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE = NO in project.pbxproj
2. Point to custom Info.plist with UIApplicationSupportsMultipleScenes: false

This disables multi-window support and restores single-window, full-screen behavior
for the game.

Verified: iPhone 17 Pro simulator - perfect full screen, no letterboxing.
Fixes GitHub Issue #1.

commit.message:
Fix strict eval function declaration leak to global scope

Strict-mode eval (both direct and indirect) should scope function
declarations locally — they must not leak to the caller's global
environment. The eval VM shared the same global_this GC pointer as
the caller, so DefineGlobal during eval execution wrote directly to
the shared object. The writeback loop correctly skipped copying to
self.globals, but global_this was already mutated.

Fix: when the writeback loop skips a strict-scoped declaration, also
remove it from global_this to undo the eval VM's direct write.

commit.message:
Add x:Code directive support for inline C# in XAML (#34715)

## Description

Implements the `x:Code` XAML directive, allowing inline C# method
definitions directly in XAML files. This complements the existing XEXPR
(C# Expressions) feature.

Fixes #34712

## What it does

`x:Code` lets you define methods inline in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage">
    <x:Code><![CDATA[
        void OnButtonClicked(object sender, EventArgs e)
        {
            // inline C# method
        }
    ]]></x:Code>
    <Button Clicked="OnButtonClicked" Text="Click me" />
</ContentPage>
```

## Constraints

- **Requires XSG (XAML Source Generator)** — not supported by Runtime
inflation or XamlC
- **Requires `EnablePreviewFeatures`** — same gate as XEXPR
- **Must be a direct child of the root element** (WPF parity)
- **Must use CDATA** for the code block content
- **Must have `x:Class`** on the root element

## Architecture

x:Code is implemented as a **third source generator pipeline** alongside
CodeBehind (CB) and InitializeComponent (IC):

```
CB → x:Code → IC
```

- `ComputeXCodeSource()` extracts code blocks from parsed XAML
- `XCodeCodeWriter` emits a bare partial class (namespace + class +
verbatim code, no usings)
- The emitted syntax trees feed into the compilation before IC runs
- All IC visitors skip x:Code elements via
`GeneratorHelpers.IsXCodeElement()`

Output hint name format: `{path}_{FileName}.xaml.xcode.cs`

## Diagnostics

| ID | Condition |
|----|-----------|
| MAUIX2012 | `EnablePreviewFeatures` not set (reused from XEXPR) |
| MAUIX2015 | x:Code not a direct child of root element |
| MAUIX2016 | x:Code used without `x:Class` |

## Tests

- **6 SourceGen unit tests** — happy path, multiple blocks, no CDATA,
diagnostics, no-op
- **4 Xaml.UnitTests** — SourceGen success, Runtime failure, XamlC
failure, MockSourceGenerator no-diagnostics

---------

commit.message:
feat: pause/resume sync at the protocol level

- SyncClient.pause(): closes WebSocket, stops auto-sync timer, sets
  paused flag that blocks connect/syncNow/handleMessage
- SyncClient.resume(): clears flag, reconnects, flushes pending edits
- React wrapper exposes pauseSync()/resumeSync() with 'paused' status
- App.tsx toggle uses pause/resume instead of disconnect/connect
- E2E test: paused client does not receive edits, resume catches up

commit.message:
fix: add UIRequiresFullScreen to force full-screen on iPhone 15+ (iOS 18+)

UILaunchScreen alone is insufficient on iOS 18+. Without UIRequiresFullScreen,
iOS assumes multitasking compatibility and constrains window size to ~60% of
screen height, causing ~240px black bars top and bottom.

UIRequiresFullScreen: true opts the app out of Split View / Slide Over and
forces the OS to size the window to full screen bounds.

Confirmed fix via iPhone 17 Pro simulator screenshot (iOS 26.2).
Fixes GitHub Issue #1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
